title: Sext
description: The sixth hour. Our Lord was nailed to the Cross at Sext. Darkness covered the earth.

sections.title: Opening
sections.latinText:
V. Deus, in adiutórium meum inténde.
R. Dómine, ad adiuvándum me festína.
Glória Patri, et Fílio, et Spirítui Sancto. Sicut erat in princípio, et nunc, et semper, et in sǽcula sæculórum. Amen. Allelúia.
sections.englishText:
V. O God, come to my assistance.
R. O Lord, make haste to help me.
Glory be to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Ghost. As it was in the beginning, is now, and ever shall be, world without end. Amen. Alleluia.

sections.title: Hymn: Rector Potens, Verax Deus
sections.latinText:
Rector potens, verax Deus,
Qui témperas rerum vices,
Splendóre mane ínstruis,
Et ígnibus merídiem:

Exstíngue flammas lítium,
Aufer calórem nóxium,
Confer salútem córporum,
Verámque pacem córdium.

Præsta, Pater piíssime,
Patríque compar Únice,
Cum Spíritu Paráclito
Regnans per omne sǽculum. Amen.
sections.englishText:
O God of truth, O Lord of might,
Who orderest time and change aright,
And send'st the early morning ray,
And light'st the glow of perfect day:

Extinguish Thou each sinful fire,
And banish every ill desire;
And while Thou keep'st the body whole,
Shed forth Thy peace upon the soul.

Almighty Father, hear our cry,
Through Jesus Christ, our Lord most high,
Who, with the Holy Ghost and Thee,
Doth live and reign eternally. Amen.

sections.title: Psalms
sections.rubric: Three sections of Psalm 118, varying by day.
sections.latinText: [Proper — three sections of Psalm 118, varying by day]
sections.englishText: [Proper — three sections of Psalm 118, varying by day]

sections.title: Short Chapter
sections.latinText:
Et replévit totam domum ubi erant sedéntes: et apparuérunt illis dispertítæ linguæ tamquam ignis, sedítque supra síngulos eórum.
R. Deo grátias.
sections.englishText:
And it filled the whole house where they were sitting: and there appeared to them parted tongues as it were of fire, and it sat upon every one of them.
R. Thanks be to God.

sections.title: Closing Prayer
sections.latinText:
V. Dóminus vobíscum. R. Et cum spíritu tuo.
Orémus.
[Proper collect of the day]
R. Amen.
V. Benedicámus Dómino. R. Deo grátias.
sections.englishText:
V. The Lord be with you. R. And with thy spirit.
Let us pray.
[Proper collect of the day]
R. Amen.
V. Let us bless the Lord. R. Thanks be to God.
